violations.text: The following food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. - ice machine, - slicer, - salad spinner The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC disposed ice and took items out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Cut melons were observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit in the grab & go open case. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cut melons disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Spray bottle with chemical and quat tablets were stored with equipment and utensils. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. PIC relocated items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Yogurt parfait packaged in the food establishment are lacking proper labels (missing establishment information and allergens). Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, allergens, the quantity of the contents, and the business name and address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back hallways (both doors) and front entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was >400 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quat sanitizer is 200 ppm. PIC refilled. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Inspector observed single-use items stored on the floor in the basement area. Items must be at least 6 inches off of floor. PIC removed from floor. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025
Inspector observed leak at dump sink near drive-thru. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025
The hand-washing sink in the bathroom was lacking proper handwashing signage. PIC made sign. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the front service counter. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. PIC replace. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

The following food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of food and debris: - can opener - mixers - ice machine - slicer - salad spinners The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Various items were observed held between 44-56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discard or placed in walk-in cooler based on time. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Working containers of various dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. PIC labeled items during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the back hallways (both doors) and front entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was >400 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quat sanitizer is 200 ppm. PIC refilled bucket. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Inspector observed scoop with handle touching food. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC removed scoop. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Inspector observed single use items stored on the floor in the basement storage area. Single use/service items must be stores at least 6 inches off the floor. PIC relocated items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025
Inspector observed leak at dump sink near drive-thru. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025
